title: Spark LinkedIn Campaign — Ken Mun Personal
description: Ken Mun personal LinkedIn campaign state. Per-account separation (CHG-0860).

cadence.missedSlotRule: Push to next available slot. If slot occupied, skip. Never post late.

pipelineDesign.principle: Draft before slot. Publish at slot. Never draft at publish time.

primary.description: Weekend batch draft — all 3 posts for the week ahead drafted on Saturday, reviewed over weekend

fallback.description: Day-before single-post drafts — only run if batch missed

publish.description: Publish-only crons — post approved+imaged content at slot time. Never draft.

activeTheme.name: Alternating: A) What AI Agents in Production Actually Look Like / B) Building AI Operations in Public

themeA.name: What AI Agents in Production Actually Look Like
themeA.note: Proven resonance (LI-W3-P1 top performer, 29 reactions). Keep for content weeks when AInchors-build angle is thin.

themeB.name: Building AI Operations in Public — Daily Lessons from CREST, L-084, L-085
themeB.note: Authentic build-in-public angle. Anchors in Sprint 7 outcomes (CREST v1.2 enforcement, 31-missing-CHG fabrication lesson L-084, long-ID detection L-085, WO-002 12h reconciliation). Voice rule still applies: NO AInchors, NO Yoda, NO agent names, NO platform internals — translate to practitioner observations only.

activeTheme.rotation: Alternate weeks: Week 1 = Theme A, Week 2 = Theme B, Week 3 = Theme A, etc. First week (Tue 16 Jun 2026) = Theme A (safer, proven voice).
activeTheme.note: Two themes, alternating weeks. Resolves the wishy-washy/consulting-speak trap Ken flagged 2026-05-26 by giving each week a clear anchor.

voice.persona: Ken Mun, CTO — first-person, direct, no fluff, practical wisdom
voice.rule: External-facing only. NO mentions of AInchors, Yoda, Nexus, agent names, or platform internals.

cancelled.title: The moment I stopped trusting the demo
cancelled.theme: Theme A — What AI Agents in Production Actually Look Like
cancelled.imagePrompt: A polished conference stage with a single spotlight illuminating a perfect demo setup — laptop, clean desk, curated props — while just outside the spotlight's edge, cables are tangled, a coffee cup is knocked over, and a monitor shows an error screen in the shadows. Split lighting: warm and controlled in the spotlight, cool and chaotic in the periphery. Editorial documentary photography, tension between illusion and reality. Square 1:1 format, no text, no logos, professional quality.
